name: Zenduty Incident Management API
what_it_does: End-to-end incident management platform with REST API for managing on-call schedules, escalation policies, incidents, and alert routing — offering PagerDuty-compatible functionality at a lower price point with full API access.
use_cases: Creating and managing incidents programmatically from monitoring systems
Querying on-call schedules to determine current responder for automated notifications
Routing alerts to correct teams based on service ownership via API
Triggering incident workflows and runbooks from agent-based systems
Syncing incident data with postmortem tools and ITSM systems
not_for: Teams fully invested in PagerDuty ecosystem with custom integrations
Simple alerting without on-call rotation management needs
Organizations requiring enterprise-grade SLA guarantees from established vendor
best_when: A team needs full incident management with on-call scheduling and API access at lower cost than PagerDuty, with similar feature parity for common SRE workflows.
avoid_when: You are deeply integrated with PagerDuty's ecosystem or need the most mature incident management platform available.

auth.notes: API token from Zenduty account. Bearer token authentication. Team-scoped access. Token management per user. Integration tokens for inbound alert sources.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $5/user/month; Pro from $15; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Significantly cheaper than PagerDuty. Free tier for small teams. Good feature parity for common SRE use cases. Growing customer base as PagerDuty alternative. YC-backed startup.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API covers incidents, escalation policies, on-call schedules, services, and integrations. Webhook support for incident events. API documentation comprehensive for core workflows. Error responses include descriptive messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Incident creation creates new incidents. Incident updates are idempotent for same values. On-call schedule queries are idempotent reads. Alert deduplication available via integration key.
known_agent_gotchas: Team UUID required in all API endpoints — agents must look up team ID first
Integration-specific API endpoints differ from REST API endpoints
On-call schedule resolution requires walking escalation policy — not single API call
Younger product than PagerDuty — some enterprise features still maturing

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token auth. SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant. US and EU data residency. YC-backed with growing security posture as product matures.
